title: Embargo Policy Ruleset
description: Per-scope policy ruleset consumed by the engine, admission gate, and console.

version.description: Schema version. Bump with a migration when breaking changes are made.

rules.description: Ordered list of policy rules. Resolution is most-specific-wins, not first-match.

scope.description: Glob or comma-separated list of globs over package names. Specificity determines which rule wins when multiple match. Examples: '@mycompany/auth', '@mycompany/*', 'express,axios', '**'.

cooldown_hours.description: Hold new versions until they are at least this many hours old. 0 = allow immediately (bypass cooldown). The cooldown window is when signals are evaluated; a version flagged during HOLD escalates to permanent DENY.

require_provenance.description: When true, deny versions without a verified SLSA/npm build-provenance attestation. Catches review-bypass and out-of-pipeline publishes.

on_hard_signal.description: Action when a hard signal fires (advisory match, typosquat, republish anomaly). 'deny' = permanent DENY; 'hold' = HOLD for manual review. Production default: deny.

fast_track.description: Exact package names exempt from this rule's cooldown and provenance requirement. Use for trusted internal packages or emergency CVE fixes. Entries must be exact names, not globs.

enabled.description: Disabled rules are skipped during resolution. Use to stage rule changes without deleting them.
